metadata.title: Why a Perfect AI Model Killed the Doorbell
metadata.notes: First embedded-ai film. Card exclusions: no Target A/B cost comparison, no edge-vs-cloud four reasons, no FPU-emulation detail — one device, one cascade, power is the punchline. The doorbell story is the textbook's teaching anecdote (unnamed company); FACTCHECK.md governs how the claims are framed. The cascade arithmetic transplants ch 1's worked numbers (15 mA active / 5 uA sleep / ~11% duty) as an illustrative calculation, never attributed to the doorbell vendor.

beats.narration_text: In late 2019, a smart doorbell that promised six months on a single charge started dying in forty-eight hours. Thousands of them. All at once.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In late twenty nineteen, a smart doorbell that promised six months on a single charge started dying in forty-eight hours. Thousands of them. All at once.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits. Slow push toward the doorbell's dark, dead lens.

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Suburban front door in flat November light, a sleek smart video doorbell mounted beside it, indicator LED dark; set shot.focus on the doorbell

beats.narration_text: This is the story of how a machine-learning model that worked — killed the product it shipped in.
beats.tts_normalized_text: This is the story of how a machine-learning model that worked — killed the product it shipped in.

card.title: WHY A PERFECT MODEL KILLED THE DOORBELL

beats.narration_text: The hardware hadn't changed. The battery hadn't changed. Three weeks earlier, a firmware update had quietly added person detection: a neural network, running on the doorbell itself.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The hardware hadn't changed. The battery hadn't changed. Three weeks earlier, a firmware update had quietly added person detection: a neural network, running on the doorbell itself.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION·ai] Product-teardown style plate: doorbell casing opened flat, board and battery visible. Remotion chips land on cue: 'HARDWARE — unchanged' (keyed to 'hardware'), 'BATTERY — unchanged' (keyed to 'battery'), terracotta chip 'FIRMWARE v2.1 — +person detection' (keyed to 'firmware update'). Degrades to clean plate.

beats.narration_text: And the team had done their homework. Model accuracy: tested. Runs on the processor: confirmed. Fits in memory: confirmed. Every box on the checklist, checked.
beats.tts_normalized_text: And the team had done their homework. Model accuracy: tested. Runs on the processor: confirmed. Fits in memory: confirmed. Every box on the checklist, checked.
beats.new_visual_element: [SCAN·prop] Designed 'INTEGRATION TEST REPORT' prop sheet (typewriter serif on aged paper, three checklist rows + one conspicuously absent fourth row of empty space). REMOTION PLANE: golden highlighter sweeps each row keyed to 'accuracy', 'processor', 'memory'; hand-drawn tick lands after each. The empty bottom of the page is the setup for B05. Degrades to hold+slow-zoom. Regions in annotations.json.

beats.narration_text: Here's what the checklist missed. Shipping AI onto a battery is a cascade of three budgets. Budget one: memory. This model fit. Pass.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Here's what the checklist missed. Shipping AI onto a battery is a cascade of three budgets. Budget one: memory. This model fit. Pass.

viz.data: Three empty budget slots appear as a vertical cascade (MEMORY / LATENCY / POWER), serif labels, hairline underlines. The MEMORY slot fills: dusty-blue demand bar rises against a charcoal budget line and stops under it. Serif stamp 'PASS' in dusty blue, keyed to the spoken 'Pass'.
viz.note: Cascade layout is the film's spine — B06 and B09 reuse this exact frame and camera. No numbers on this bar; the shape is the claim (ch 1: weights+activations 240 KB vs 256 KB is the textbook's example, kept off-screen per exclusions).

beats.narration_text: Budget two: time. Operations divided by processor speed — a couple of seconds per look. For a doorbell, acceptable. Pass.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Budget two: time. Operations divided by processor speed — a couple of seconds per look. For a doorbell, acceptable. Pass.

viz.data: Same cascade frame. LATENCY slot fills: dusty-blue bar rises to just under the budget line, label 'seconds per look'. Second serif 'PASS' stamp keyed to the spoken 'Pass'. MEMORY bar remains, dimmed.
viz.note: Transform-don't-cut from B05 — the cascade persists, only the active slot changes.

beats.narration_text: Budget three, nobody ran. Every look holds the processor awake — and seconds of thinking, times looks per minute, is a duty cycle. The fraction of its life spent awake.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Budget three, nobody ran. Every look holds the processor awake — and seconds of thinking, times looks per minute, is a duty cycle. The fraction of its life spent awake.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits. Breaks the GRAPHIC run before the arithmetic beats.

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION·ai] Macro plate of the doorbell's circuit board, processor centered. Remotion overlay: a horizontal awake/asleep timeline strip across the lower third — terracotta AWAKE blocks against a long dusty-blue ASLEEP floor — drawing left-to-right keyed to 'seconds of thinking'; label chip 'DUTY CYCLE = awake ÷ total' lands on the spoken 'duty cycle'. Degrades to clean plate.

beats.narration_text: Awake is expensive — thousands of times the current of sleep. A few seconds of inference every thirty, and the average draw jumps from microamps to milliamps.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Awake is expensive — thousands of times the current of sleep. A few seconds of inference every thirty, and the average draw jumps from microamps to milliamps.

viz.data: Two-magnitude comparison on the newsprint ground: a barely-visible dusty-blue sliver labeled 'asleep · microamps' beside a terracotta column labeled 'awake · milliamps' (log-scale honesty: axis breaks marked with hand-drawn tildes). Then a time-weighted average line sweeps across the B07-style strip and settles far above the sliver, golden highlighter bar under 'average'.
viz.note: CLAIM CHECK — magnitudes from ch 1 worked example: 15 mA active vs 5 uA sleep (×3000), 3.4 s inference per 30 s window → ~11% duty → ~1.7 mA average. Numbers stay off-screen as digits; the ratio drawing must remain faithful to ×1000-scale.

beats.narration_text: That's the murder weapon. A battery sized for a sleeping device now feeds one that's awake a tenth of the time. Six months becomes two days.
beats.tts_normalized_text: That's the murder weapon. A battery sized for a sleeping device now feeds one that's awake a tenth of the time. Six months becomes two days.

viz.data: Return to the cascade frame from B05/B06. The POWER slot fills: terracotta demand bar rises PAST the charcoal budget line and keeps going. Hand-drawn orange ring circles the overshoot keyed to 'murder weapon'. Right margin: 'SIX MONTHS' in serif struck through with an ink line, 'TWO DAYS' stamped beneath, keyed to the spoken words.
viz.note: The card's visual object — the waterfall's final bar missing the line. 48 hours vs 6 months is the chapter's anecdote framing; ratio ~90×.

beats.narration_text: Twelve thousand doorbells came back in the first month. By December, the feature was rolled back. Deleted.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Twelve thousand doorbells came back in the first month. By December, the feature was rolled back. Deleted.

viz.data: Isotype grid: charcoal squares, one square = 500 returned units, 24 squares counting up in reading order keyed to the beat's audio window, caption 'one square = 500 returned units'. On 'rolled back', a terracotta ink X strikes a small 'PERSON DETECTION' label chip.
viz.note: CLAIM CHECK — 12,000 first-month returns and December rollback are the textbook anecdote's figures (see FACTCHECK.md); isotype unit chosen so count stays ≤30 marks.

beats.narration_text: Here's the uncomfortable part: nothing failed. The model was accurate. The hardware ran it perfectly. The system did exactly what it was designed to do. The design was wrong.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Here's the uncomfortable part: nothing failed. The model was accurate. The hardware ran it perfectly. The system did exactly what it was designed to do. The design was wrong.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits. The moral beat.

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION·ai] The B01 doorbell again, closer, lens dark. Remotion chips land in sequence: 'MODEL — correct' (dusty blue, keyed to 'accurate'), 'HARDWARE — correct' (dusty blue, keyed to 'perfectly'), 'DESIGN — wrong' (terracotta, keyed to the final 'wrong'). Degrades to clean plate.

beats.narration_text: Before a model ships onto a battery, run three budgets: memory, latency, power. The third one is the one that kills.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Before a model ships onto a battery, run three budgets: memory, latency, power. The third one is the one that kills.
